title: grangea aegyptiaca
intro: Grangea aegyptiaca, also known as Egyptian tea or Egyptian willowerb, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as a diuretic. The health benefits of Grangea aegyptiaca include its potential to reduce the risk of kidney stones, manage symptoms of menopause, and alleviate gastrointestinal issues. The plant can be consumed as a tea, dried and powdered for capsules, or infused into oils for topical use, showcasing its versatility in herbal preparations.
